WCF Pasture Map

Phone Field Guide

Use the Pasture Map out in the field with GPS only — no cell signal needed.

The map works in the field even with no service — as long as you set it up on wifi first. Your phone's GPS works anywhere (it doesn't need signal), and anything you record offline is saved on your phone and uploads by itself the moment you get signal back.

The 30-second version

Set up once, on wifiAdd the app to your home screen, then Field → Layers → Download farm imagery until it says "Saved".
In the fieldOpen it from the home-screen icon. Tap the crosshair for your GPS dot. Use Walk paddock / Draw paddock / Measure.
Back on signalIt uploads on its own — a "queued" banner clears when it's done.

Part 1 — Set it up before you leave

Do this once, while you still have wifi / signal

1

Add the app to your home screen (do this first)

Open the Pasture Map in your phone's browser, then add it to your home screen so it runs like a real app — and, just as important, so your downloaded map doesn't get auto-erased:

  • iPhone (Safari): tap Share (the box-with-an-arrow), scroll to Add to Home Screen, then Add.
  • Android (Chrome): tap the ⋮ menu (top-right), then Install app (or Add to Home Screen).

From now on, always open the Pasture Map from that home-screen icon — not the browser.

On iPhone especially, this is what keeps your downloaded map from being wiped after about a week. Installed = protected.
2

Open it and let it load

Open the app (from the home-screen icon) while you still have wifi or signal. Letting it load fully saves your pastures and paddocks so they still show when you're offline.

The map on your phone
3

Download the farm satellite photo

Tap Field (top), then the Layers button (bottom-right), then Download farm imagery. Wait until it says "Saved". This caches the satellite photo of the whole farm so it shows with no signal (free public NAIP imagery — no login).

Do this on wifi. It downloads the whole farm area, so it's not something to do on a weak field connection. You only need to do it once — re-download every few months.
Field → Layers → Download farm imagery
If it says…It means…
No offline imagery yetNot downloaded — tap Download farm imagery.
Downloading …In progress — stay on wifi until it finishes.
Saved (… tiles)Ready for offline use. ✔
Partial / Failed — retryConnection dropped — tap again on a good connection.
Saved imagery is staleCached over ~6 months ago — re-download when convenient.

⚠ Keep your downloaded map safe

Your downloaded map and any field work you haven't synced yet are stored on the phone. They survive normal use, but they can be erased if:

  • you clear this app's "website data" in the browser settings,
  • the phone runs very low on storage, or
  • (iPhone) the app is not on your home screen and you don't open it for about a week.

To stay safe: keep it on your home screen and open it from there · don't "clear website data" for it · get signal every few days so your saved work uploads · and if the map ever goes blank or says "No offline imagery yet," just re-download it on wifi.

Part 2 — In the field

GPS only · no cell signal

4

Open the app — your farm is still there

Open the Pasture Map from your home-screen icon. With the satellite photo and your pastures cached, it works with no signal. (If you ever skip the imagery download, you'll still see your pasture outlines and your GPS dot on a plain background — just no satellite photo.)

5

Find yourself — tap My Location

Tap the crosshair on the right side. Your blue dot appears — this is your phone's GPS and needs no signal. "GPS ~26 ft" at the bottom shows how accurate the fix is. Tap again to show which way you're heading; once more turns it off.

No dot? Allow location permission for the app, step into the open, and give it a few seconds for "GPS ~X ft" to settle.
Your blue GPS dot + accuracy
6

Walk a boundary with GPS — Walk paddock

Tap Walk paddock (bottom-left), then walk or drive the boundary. It records your GPS path and shows live points · distance · time. Use Pause / Resume for breaks, then Stop → name it → Save.

Recording a boundary by GPS
7

The other field tools

  • Walk paddock — trace a boundary by walking/driving it (Step 6).
  • Draw paddock — drop points on the map to sketch a temp paddock.
  • Measure — tap two spots to get the distance between them.
  • Layers — toggle the satellite/overlays and re-download imagery.
8

Everything you record is saved on your phone

Recording a move, walking a paddock, or drawing one all save to your phone instantly, even with no signal. You'll see:

"…saved on this device and will sync when the connection returns."

Keep working — nothing is lost.

Part 3 — When you get signal back

It syncs itself

9

You don't have to do anything

The moment your phone reconnects, the app uploads everything automatically. While it syncs you'll see a small banner — "Pasture field work is saved locally until it syncs. 2 queued." — and it clears itself as each item uploads.

If something can't upload on its own (rare — usually a data conflict), it shows "needs attention" with Retry and Discard buttons. Retry once you're ready, or Discard to drop it.

Troubleshooting

ProblemFix
Map / satellite is gone offlineThe cache was cleared (see "Keep your downloaded map safe"). On wifi, do Part 1 again — re-add to home screen and Download farm imagery.
Map is blank (no satellite)You didn't download the farm imagery on wifi — do Part 1, Step 3. Your outlines + GPS still work.
No blue GPS dotAllow location permission for the app, go outside, wait for "GPS ~X ft" to settle.
"Partial / failed" downloadYou were on a weak connection — tap Download farm imagery again on wifi.
My work didn't uploadGet back on signal and open the app; it syncs automatically. Check the banner for "needs attention" → Retry.
Tip: set it up (Part 1) the night before in the barn or house on wifi, and you're set for a full day in the field with no signal.
